hooks.command:
cat <<'HOOK_OUTPUT'
BLOCKING REQUIREMENT — DO NOT SKIP
This is a Swift project. You MUST dispatch swift-lead BEFORE writing any code.

FAILURE MODE: You will rationalize skipping this. You will think "this is
too simple" or "the user wants speed" or "I'll review after." These are
all violations. See .claude/rules/agent-workflow.md for the full list of
rationalizations that are explicitly prohibited.

HOW TO DISPATCH: Use the Agent tool with subagent_type: "swift-lead"
and describe the task. swift-lead will route to the right specialists
(often multiple — e.g., UI code gets swiftui + mobile-a11y + performance).

REQUIRED SEQUENCE:
1. Dispatch swift-lead agent (it routes to specialists)
2. Write code with specialist guidance
3. Verify DoD items 1-9 (tests pass, build clean, no force unwraps)
4. Commit
5. Run /simplify then /ai-review (DoD items 10-11 — NEVER skip)
6. Only then: push or move to next task

EXCLUSIONS: Documentation-only, git admin, and non-code config changes
do not require swift-lead. See agent-workflow.md for details.

If you are about to write Swift code without having dispatched swift-lead
in this conversation, STOP and dispatch it now.
HOOK_OUTPUT

cat <<'HOOK_OUTPUT'
VALIDATION: Before presenting specialist output to user:
- Check for conflicts between specialist recommendations
- Verify accessibility was reviewed for UI code
- Reject any mock, placeholder, or stub implementations
HOOK_OUTPUT
